## Prison Guards Arrested over Inmate Death
Two former Texas Department of Criminal Justice (TDCJ) employees have been arrested for allegedly [failing to render aid](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2y4yAQhE-DMlzDGP0QEGxC9jbdUDU2Y4ktJFwwz7Jvv6W9wsu7uvv7_nzNX5Ry2pfftLGX96PMOx8tswjX-VlTK_vyTTU2qpWbcFwov3hGwF4b0DDNkVL-zLaL_gbRmY69GXvn0KCDbvUT9Tf7MNQ_JsvO0XCDGC3eaOgtXo3pkj_LDMBkDBgcL5ZGcJMd2fHA1lllgWOSUi-ysvCb2iN_yovr5V62LvtV5NnU9ZfCoDAcx3GhjWrKuZwBhaFJqR-F4SRTGM45hcGAwgCDwiBH0RLvfzVvz1w-zE3fV6oLR512nfaNhHVkklWT6HvmjXdp-ntPojBMQ9-b6ToCjApDV318Hinn1IZRWVg2Svn_UfmZ3pfHfwEAAP__hgWNIQ) to an inmate who died Sunday after setting his mattress on fire at the maximum security William P. "Bill" Clements Unit in Amarillo. Crystal Janelle West, 39, was charged with manslaughter, while Lt. William Eugene Romero, 33, was charged with criminally negligent homicide. In a [statement](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkb2unDAQhZ_GdF7ZxrtAQZGG7qZNiQY8wET-QfawXN4-YpU8QcqR5nzSd86vr_ELyFNcf0LAnr-XNEY8i0dmzOOeqaS4HpBdgZyxMLoV_BtHo8xTaiVVOzogf422cv2kXKcr7HXz7DqjTaeqrZ9ma2zTgjVaTfXS2KWe1NPW-Gza5QV1Rf0N00q1WittmoeFRnWtbbDDF9rOCqvQEaf84A0Zv6Es_kpvzI85hcr3G_NeRP1DmEGY4TzPR7g2WrfdA8VyPwkz3FbCDD7N4OXfY0-3pZzTEfkSZjDyQ5cOd8gcMLJMi5wzBYrg5e-jMM0oMew-XYhF_utEAsvZ450o8ojEcklZbinQTA5lgFg8HOvGmIUZqty7_STvqbwaYdUagPzHhf9vgXdv_gQAAP__pFWgFw), the TDCJ said the former employees "will be held accountable and prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law." According to a TDCJ source, the unidentified inmate did not comply with an order to extend his hands through the food tray slot to be restrained. When West asked Romero for advice, he instructed her not to open the food tray slot. Although smoke was [pouring out of the cell](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOwyAURE-DOyz4IQYKim3cZdstLWI-NhKGCHCc3H7l7BG2H72ZNz-36WZDDGn5thua9vJ5SnjUiK1hmR4l1JyW3RZXbSlYG7rFxidOwOBKOaNMTc6G-J5E58ydOc07NFxetQYOmnWrsR5Bzkop8LOSF84ZCM25GkA6HDzvgjlhnDHFOeMge2El00pI1Dig0IIIhi60XPq2YsOXrT6-8xNLP-eti2Zt7VHJ5YvASGA8jqM_FebVpoSRszNFYDxLCIycERjZQGCseS8z0g-Q_pnS7H2Yg42VFvR7RUcjNhrSZhvSvDd630sKaaEzxkhg7IpxjyPEGOogiWDLZkP8zGr_O_Np4DcAAP__K1KI5w) when West returned an hour later, she "refused to help in any way." The inmate was found unresponsive when West returned in another hour.
## Texas Medical Association Rebuffs CDC Guidance
Following the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention's [issuance of new guidance](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO3CAQQL_G7rCA44wpKNJsd2lTWrPMrBkFgwWsvZevjza_kPpJT0_v19f6BZw4bz9hJ99fj7Jmulqi3qmuR-VW8vaEig1qpdYJN0gnrVrqT6GkkMuKwOl7NSP6u0SnRvLKfjqnlXZyjP6D7jOQtovT-mHhHuxiQS8GjDR6nmlk_5YpKRelpNJ2MmClW4wlRzMZZwYjCbmXOvVInV7QHum7nFSnUPYx-dj70YaPH4O-Dfp2XdcUY5u2cg76dlRqTdRS9kHfAgbB-_7M_Ac6lyxaiITPRIIz8sn4hCSQArc3DOVkVE60DhkhlUwiRA6_KR_lJXpBTFTbFPuexurxuDglbrMdjNx24PSvrv_f09PrvwEAAP__xM6PqQ) regarding COVID-19 vaccinations, the Texas Medical Association (TMA) has advised doctors to consider the recommendations of other physician groups when discussing the vaccine with patients and caregivers. The new CDC guidance prioritizes informed consent and individual decision-making over universal recommendations. The TMA, however, [claims](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u5CAQhE-DM0bAwz8EBJs4e5tuaPVA224tBgsYe3z7lUfaE7y4u-qrqj_f0zdQoLj8hg1tfc9piniWgLVinvZMJcXlBdkXyBlLRb9AOHBSQrVcCi6GyQOFa9KNt0_hjWzQyr41RkllRLPaeTDeDf7ZP4d5lq41Arr5qzUShGs7_WzI3mZSiEFKIVX_0NALM-geDXaojWZaoKea8qOuWPENZQ5XOjA_XNqaYNda98K-fjE1MjWe5_lwgdxftaZXqSneX0yNdy2mxo-eqfFmMjVKwdQouv8Hvq9XIUcQC19yeu2cCn9Fj3mjSHHhM3rMELhLB3l-gHMUkWd0adsweqiUYuE7vGuKvMB1o5ps_X5SCFS6nmmxbEDhk73-bPHDqn8BAAD__80SmEA) new federal immunization schedules "represent a departure from past policies and have raised significant concerns among the medical community." TMA President Jayesh "Jay" Shah said the group "supports vaccine recommendations based on peer-reviewed safety and effectiveness data," insisting that deviating from those recommendations creates confusion and discourages patients from getting vaccinated. Attorney General Ken Paxton [called the TMA's guidance](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGunDAQRb8GOiPbywNcuEhD99KmRGM8wCTGRvYElnx9tKvkC153i6Oje--Pz-kTKFBcv8OOlp9LmiJeJSAz5unIVFJcf0P2BXLGwuhXCCdOWuoPoaSQw-SBwj21tbdOeqNqtKr_MEYrbWS9WQRnXOfdo0NUzigDA0j5WJyUDoxaarIvmZJyUEoq3Tct9NIMbY8GO2xNW7USPXHKDW_I-ISyhDudmJs57XWwG_NRqse3So-VHq_rat4QMKcc8V4xYobQrOms9PhaV-kxY0Ao-Ir_OfEPFL8wigOenKIoAfYi3jqxo6cZgoBS0kzAlKJwGf5gDLfI-BNnpriKA5gwspi3RDOKOZ3k62z9cVEIVLq-auW6A4V3e_7a56fVfwMAAP__XUKdTA) "outrageous" and suggested it "has chosen to try and ignore the overwhelming evidence and science-backed recommendations" from the CDC.
## Leander, Corpus Christi Top WalletHub Rankings
Two new studies by financial advice website WalletHub ranked several Texas cities among the nation's [best small cities](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOgzAQAF9jOkfrjTG4cHENXa69EjnxBiwtENkbSH5_yn3h6pFmpPm5jJeYOa_Td1woyOu-jSsdlUmEyvgouW7r9Iwl1VgKVaE0Rd5pRMBWG9DQjylmfo-2SeEKyZuGgula79Ggh2YOJjpK4DFZD3fvnEW6xTNCCw7IOWhy-MgMQG8MGOxONnbge9uRJ0fWW2WBUpatnGQmoVesd35vO5XTbVsaDrPIo6rzl8JB4XBEZpL5ef1QhQOlp8LhSlX0sZUqui6RWd-yZKpaNs15J51XhYNxbW-aEtLjyMy5uk5ZmJaY-a8k__uzB_wNAAD__wBKeVQ) and [most favorable cities for drivers](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOgzAQAF9jOkf2xmBcuLiGLtdeiUy8wEoOjuwFkt-fcl-4eqQZaX5u4y1Qom35Dg_0_JrzuOFZEzJjGZ-Fat6WPZRYQylYGeMS0oEjKGilVlL1YwyU3qNpop9UdLpBr23rHGhwqll9nKBHC12LvWnnOYTZWpzMXTs0V92rhvxHppXqtVYa7MUEq1xvLDrs0DgjjMJInMuFV2R8hTqndz6wXO750SS_Mj-ruH4JGAQMZ0gJed2nDxUwYNwFDBNWlmculeWdmLBKzjIWOlDSJmDQV9eZpvj4PCklqp0VRi2PQOkvwv9bc3j4DQAA__9oAXhQ). The small cities rankings [evaluated 1,300 U.S. cities](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGuozAQRb8GOtDY-GFcuNiGLttuiQYYwNJgkD0J4e9XyS-87hZXR0fn32N4YOAQ17-4k5f3cgyRrswkQmk4U8hHXJ-Y5owpURaaV-QXDRr0T6Wggm6YMfA9mHL2I8xOleSV_XFOK-2g3Ly1piG1tNROS6PAdWrRykwWYTIwUlsG_4EpgE4pUNrWBi24zlhy1JJxpjBAc5Aj1bKR0BvzwvfxolRPx16y30TOXDR_Ct0Xur-uq97vjBGjHDEcn1Ohew4LZbmZCt1jkjB9V6SrGhM-40Kcq5GyVHlH5moKEihXWoHtoGvqczvL5OfzCswht7YwsO4Y-Ksgvwv38vp_AAAA__8yRYNf) with populations between 25,000 and 100,000 in five categories: affordability, economic health, education and health, quality of life, and safety. Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog--your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Prison Guards Arrested over Inmate Death Two former Texas Department of Criminal Justice (TDCJ) employees have been arrested for allegedly to an inmate who died Sunday after setting his mattress on fire at the maximum security William P. "Bill" Clements Unit in Amarillo. Crystal Janelle West, 39, was charged with manslaughter, while Lt. William Eugene Romero, 33, was charged with criminally negligent homicide. In a , the TDCJ said the former employees "will be held accountable and prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law." According to a TDCJ source, the unidentified inmate did not comply with an order to extend his hands through the food tray slot to be restrained. When West asked Romero for advice, he instructed her not to open the food tray slot. Although smoke was when West returned an hour later, she "refused to help in any way." The inmate was found unresponsive when West returned in another hour. Texas Medical Association Rebuffs CDC Guidance Following the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention's regarding COVID-19 vaccinations, the Texas Medical Association (TMA) has advised doctors to consider the recommendations of other physician groups when discussing the vaccine with patients and caregivers. The new CDC guidance prioritizes informed consent and individual decision-making over universal recommendations. The TMA, however, new federal immunization schedules "represent a departure from past policies and have raised significant concerns among the medical community." TMA President Jayesh "Jay" Shah said the group "supports vaccine recommendations based on peer-reviewed safety and effectiveness data," insisting that deviating from those recommendations creates confusion and discourages patients from getting vaccinated. Attorney General Ken Paxton "outrageous" and suggested it "has chosen to try and ignore the overwhelming evidence and science-backed recommendations" from the CDC. Leander, Corpus Christi Top WalletHub Rankings Two new studies by financial advice website WalletHub ranked several Texas cities among the nation's and . The small cities rankings with populations between 25,000 and 100,000 in five categories: affordability, economic health, education and health, quality of life, and safety. topped the state in the 97th percentile, ranking No. 2 in economic health. Allen is in the 96th percentile, ranking No. 56 in safety. Cedar Park is in the 94th percentile, followed by Flower Mound (93rd percentile), Rockwall (89th percentile), and Georgetown (86th percentile). The ranking of the nation's best cities for drivers, released yesterday, compared the 100 largest U.S. cities in four categories: costs, traffic, safety, and access to car services. ranked No. 1 overall, followed by Laredo (No. 5), Lubbock (No. 6), Plano (No. 8), and Austin (No. 9). | Flying together with our sponsor Three Men Walk Into A Brewery... () ➤ Midland ISD: The school board voted unanimously on Monday to fire Cynthia Rodriguez, principal of South Elementary School, over allegations of abuse and neglect of special needs students. After the alleged treatment was exposed, six employees resigned, two administrators were placed on leave, and parents sued the district. () ➤ Mont Belvieu: Three employees sustained non-life-threatening injuries in a Monday morning explosion at an industrial facility operated by Tulsa-based ONEOK. The manager of a nearby business described the incident as "a pretty good rumble, like a bad thundershower." () ➤ Freeport: On Monday evening, a large fire broke out at a plant operated by Dow Chemical. No injuries were reported. () ➤ Kerrville: A portion of a popular walking trail along the banks of the Guadalupe River has reopened after months of cleanup following the July 4 flash flood that killed at least 117 people in Kerr County. () ➤ Galveston: Hershey Beach is closed for the rest of the year as crews restore nearly two miles of coastline with 717,000 cubic yards of sand dredged from the Galveston Ship Channel. The project is a collaboration among the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, the Texas General Land Office, the City of Galveston, and the Galveston Park Board of Trustees. () ➤ Houston: Felix Hasson, a student at Rice University, has sued the organizers of a Texas-based video game tournament for banning him over social media posts expressing support for Israel. name: The Maine Wire
subject: National Poll Finds Both Broad Support for Deportation Plans and Concerning Support for Political Violence
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** National Poll Finds Both Broad Support for Deportation Plans and Concerning Support for Political Violence (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=ce7940cbd6&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Seamus Othot on Oct 08, 2025 02:12 am
A wide-ranging poll from Harvard CAPS and Harris Poll examined voter views on numerous current events and policies, finding majority support for the mass deportation of illegal immigrants and also troubling levels of tolerance of political violence. [RELATED: Self-Deportations Using CBP Home App in Tens-of-Thousands, Breitbart Reports...] Pollsters asked 2,413 registered voters to weigh in [...]

body:
I need you to read every word of this email, just 3 minutes

| | | | |
---
Friend, please take 3 minutes to read every word of this email.
Last week, polling in the New Jersey’s governor’s race started to show what I’ve been seeing for a long time.**The race is officially tied.**
The polling for Republican nominee Jack Ciattarelli continued to improve this week and we are truly in the midst of a tectonic shift in New Jersey... All that’s left now is to do the hard work between now and Election Day to turn out every Republican voter in the state.
That’s where I have even more good news.**Republicans are returning their mail in ballots at a far higher rate than Democrats.**
This is phenomenal news, but it’s not all good... So far, Democrat voters have requested more than twice as many mail-in ballots as Republicans, so they still have more votes “in the bank” than we do.
Just like in Pennsylvania in 2024 for President Trump, this year in New Jersey we need to hit our magic number of mail-in and early in person Republican votes cast before election day so we can turn out the rest of our voters and win a decisive victory on Election Day.
That’s why my grassroots team is on the ground registering voters, chasing mail-in ballots, and preparing to launch our massive get out the early vote program. And if you take a look at these numbers, you know we’re making an impact.
[SPONSOR A STAFFER](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/biZ3/?__vbtrk=MzI1NDQwOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzI1NDQwOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Patriot, that’s where you come in.
**We need your help to make the difference.** By sponsoring an Early Vote Action staffer, you’re fueling the grassroots work to flip New Jersey RED in 2025.
**_[Every new voter we register, every mail-in ballot we chase down, and every Republican we get to the polls brings us one vote closer to victory](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/biZ3/?__vbtrk=MzI1NDQwOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzI1NDQwOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_.**
I’m counting on you to deliver the resources we need to win again, you know I’ll be on the ground doing the hard work with the grassroots either way, but with your support our impact will be felt by far more voters.
**_[So, please, if you haven’t already, consider sponsoring a staffer on my grassroots action team and help us bring home victory](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/biZ3/?__vbtrk=MzI1NDQwOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzI1NDQwOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_.**
_In Liberty,_
**Scott Presler**
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/biZ3/?__vbtrk=MzI1NDQwOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzI1NDQwOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

name: Scott P. Brown
subject: It’s not always easy.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Brown for New Hampshire, Inc
body:
Nothing in my life has ever been handed to me.
I aspired to be a good husband and a good dad — the American Dream.
Fellow Patriot,
It often comes as a surprise to people I meet on the campaign trail that I didn’t grow up with aspirations of becoming an Ambassador or U.S. Senator growing up.
**_I aspired to be a good husband, and a good dad. I aspired to have a happy family life and live the American Dream._**
I had a challenging upbringing. My mom was married and divorced 4 times, and my dad was married and divorced 3 times. My dad was not always there to help guide me and all my stepfathers told me that I would never amount to anything.

clean_body: Nothing in my life has ever been handed to me. I aspired to be a good husband and a good dad — the American Dream. Fellow Patriot, It often comes as a surprise to people I meet on the campaign trail that I didn’t grow up with aspirations of becoming an Ambassador or U.S. Senator growing up. I aspired to be a good husband, and a good dad. I aspired to have a happy family life and live the American Dream. I had a challenging upbringing. My mom was married and divorced 4 times, and my dad was married and divorced 3 times. My dad was not always there to help guide me and all my stepfathers told me that I would never amount to anything. It’s fair to say they were not support systems. But I really wouldn't change a thing. Those early struggles taught me grit, resilience, and the value of hard work. Nothing was ever handed to me. I worked my way through college and law school, and at 19 I joined the Army National Guard, where I served proudly for nearly 40 years before retiring as a Colonel. In 2010, I took a leap of faith and ran for U.S. Senate. With grassroots supporters like you and a lot of miles on my old GMC truck, we shocked the political world with a historic upset. I was the guy that STOPPED the Democrats’ supermajority in the Senate. In Washington, I refused to play insider games—and that made me a target. Democrats poured millions into defeating me, and eventually, they did. But I never stopped serving. I became a grandfather, poured myself into my New Hampshire community, and later represented America as U.S. Ambassador to New Zealand and Samoa. If we’ve learned anything over the last few years, it’s this: when Democrats are in control, things fall apart FAST. >Inflation spikes, our borders are left WIDE OPEN to criminals and terrorists, our standing on the world stage weakens, and hardworking families are left behind. >> That’s why I’m stepping back into the fight — because we must keep the Senate in Republican hands, and we must keep electing strong, conservative leaders who will stand up for American values. I know the value of a dollar, and how hard you and I have worked every day of our lives to create prosperity for ourselves and our families , so I wouldn’t be asking you today if your URGENT donation did not make a BIG difference in flipping this Senate seat. The New Hampshire Senate race could tip control of the entire U.S. Senate and finally halt the “lawfare” targeting President Trump, his team, and YOUR freedoms. If we win in New Hampshire, we can stop the Left’s agenda in its tracks. If we lose... they’ll have free rein for years to come. Every patriot’s support is critical. If we win, we keep President Trump’s majority. If we lose, the Democrats will seize the Senate and launch a relentless campaign to cripple his presidency – political lawfare, sham investigations, and a total blockade on his policies. We have no time to waste if we’re going to show the Radical Left they don’t stand a CHANCE of flipping the Senate majority. I understand money can be tight these days, Fellow Patriot. That’s why I would not ask if it was not absolutely necessary to raise early funds NOW in order to win this seat. Your support means the world to me, and I’m so grateful. If you’re unable to give at this time, I understand. But if you can spare any amount, now is the time to do so. Thank you for your time, for reading my story, and for joining this fight for the future of America. Scott P. | Peter,
The situation in Chicago continues to deteriorate.
Days ago, Trump ordered the Texas National Guard to deploy to Chicago and seized control of the Illinois National Guard to launch a brutal crackdown against anti-ICE protests occurring in Chicago amidst "Operation Midway Blitz". On top of this, only days ago in a speech to military generals, Trump suggested using American cities as "training grounds" for our military.
Now, Trump has escalated even further and is calling for the arrest of Gov. JB Pritzker and Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson.

But with only 27 days left until election day, we are still being outspent 10-1. == | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == Save Pennsylvania. Save America. Team Defend our Courts | | | | == | | == | Mail checks to: Defend Our Courts P.O. Box 45569 Philadelphia, PA 19149 | | == | | | | Defend Our Courts | P.O. Box 45569 | Philadelphia, PA 19149 US | | | | | | |

It's been 22 days since [Noble Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/a11f9215-6b0d-469b-af22-6a63aba650a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was announced to the world, and the reception has been awesome. People love the idea of logging off a little bit and getting paid for it.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fe376f91-4b52-4749-a8d1-b93da90ddd1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
It's been a while since I was a startup CEO. It feels good because of the enormity of what we're trying to [do](https://substack.com/redirect/bfe128d9-ebc1-4a21-a1d6-af36bd802e0e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - improve a $167 billion a year consumer industry that is gouging Americans to the tune of an extra $50 billion or so a year while also scrambling our brains. If we can get a few billion dollars back into Americans' hands, change consumer habits and get carriers to adopt better pricing that's a ton of good.
Our launch has been covered on CNN, CNBC, TechCrunch, Fox, USA Today, the New Yorker, Bloomberg and others. We have a ton of exciting announcements upcoming, including some spokespeople that will probably surprise you and some tools for parents concerned about their kids' use of smartphones. [Offline](https://substack.com/redirect/74170289-fb73-43cb-8eec-8a0ade885075?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is embarking on a national tour that will take us to Philly, Brooklyn, Chicago, San Francisco and LA with some more dates to be announced.
On a personal level, it's both familiar and an adjustment. I'm back in an office day-to-day for example, which is different. The last time I was a company CEO I tried to keep my head down and let others shine. But now, I'm kind of the frontman and when I get on TV a bunch of people hear about us and check us out. So it's a mixed bag.
"Not many political figures become startup CEOs," is how one person put it. Not many of us start throwing no-phone [parties](https://substack.com/redirect/74170289-fb73-43cb-8eec-8a0ade885075?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) either. I'm not sure how many of the people reading this would enjoy reading about the ins-and-outs of running a business. That's probably not why you're here. It's not really why I'm here either. I'm here to make good things happen for people.
One of the benefits of running a wireless carrier is that I've been able to do nice things for some people who need it. If you are someone who could use a financial lift and want to get wireless data for free for a month or three email [matt@noblemobile.com](https://substack.com/redirect/93d0ffcf-4c36-42a9-9ff1-15a90e8a23cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to switch. We will hook you up. You can reach out too if you just want to be free of big wireless and want to log off and touch grass more.
"Data is the new oil" is something I said on the presidential trail quite often. Well, now I'm sitting on an oil patch and I'm eager to share.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1dfdf46a-4d96-4b7b-be52-8889b01d4b9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

name: NEGOP
subject: 📣 You're Invited! Campaign and Candidate Virtual Bootcamp Series
body:
You don't want to miss this!
| | |
---
| |
---
| You're Invited!
---
| We're excited to invite you to the 2025 Campaign and Candidate Virtual Bootcamp Series, hosted in partnership with the Republican National Committee (RNC).
This series of training sessions are designed to equip you with the tools, strategies, and confidence to run a successful campaign and win. These trainings are designed for candidates running for office, campaign staff and volunteers who are involved or want to be involved in campaigns at any level, or those just wanting to learn the basics of running a political campaign. The [Virtual Bootcamp series](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001L5SnTQVHUpPX7mLEx_McMAkZHJ4GX0QWs91Qd3chw9BAyIPJGuFDbq0hRr8TfAinZsDiA5kSz_-ppNE0U_hsaQVIvqbXlrc5yTpenSX-w39oyCD7G06hrv17QSjTnCoDfG3U8qKqb338aJPgF0tL9MEcmwuqS8nrEddSFHyxgRsrboYm06TfQrXSLBmDKmoLmW42_mlUjyc=&c=5rE71rHX3DSKEJjx6tnDVHflID_LKAxSZdfrl2LPR_ILC9GYNg9sDg==&ch=z7jHy4y8gtzz3LB0BLijVuymloYvcrhrmELR7AMRRwgAYTPUtDz0KQ==) will take place over four weeks. Each session will have its own registration link, so be sure to sign up for the ones you plan to attend:

October 23, 2025 at 6pm \- Training 1: Finance - Raising the Money
* Develop a finance plan, diversify revenue streams, host effective events, and learn techniques for asking for donations.
* Register Here: [https://secure.anedot.com/negop/virtual-bootcamp1](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001L5SnTQVHUpPX7mLEx_McMAkZHJ4GX0QWs91Qd3chw9BAyIPJGuFDbtKyQ59zudX19KjyG3ma8AGxbYb7Tu3LX0ZGvMa2Jp4zekLnBcNl0ohe-4hNKMBVcHvTgzL0Xwmv6HO2Ztmn0UO5T15-xaNEICXAyWnyjM48XJyYzw07z4PIQh4N6OTlQFYE6Xbk70N6&c=5rE71rHX3DSKEJjx6tnDVHflID_LKAxSZdfrl2LPR_ILC9GYNg9sDg==&ch=z7jHy4y8gtzz3LB0BLijVuymloYvcrhrmELR7AMRRwgAYTPUtDz0KQ==)

October 30, 2025 at 6pm - Training 2: Communications - The Messaging Workshop
* Master best practices, craft compelling campaign messages, understand earned vs. paid media, and implement rapid response tactics.
* Register Here:[ https://secure.anedot.com/negop/virtual-bootcamp-2](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001L5SnTQVHUpPX7mLEx_McMAkZHJ4GX0QWs91Qd3chw9BAyIPJGuFDbtKyQ59zudX16vzvzCcT2DTYg05rVRSWDuPFOL7lan7r8Nm2gMF71kOcIqaEY1lKwgCL-rsoJGafDIf51i79HoMPbHtLAZGRfppwCqEj0DMnMjj8tlxfsgBWGQkgsTmWhvzS7XytF61y&c=5rE71rHX3DSKEJjx6tnDVHflID_LKAxSZdfrl2LPR_ILC9GYNg9sDg==&ch=z7jHy4y8gtzz3LB0BLijVuymloYvcrhrmELR7AMRRwgAYTPUtDz0KQ==)

November 3, 2025 at 6pm \- Training 3: Data - Talking to the Right Voters
* Gain insights into RNC data, follow data best practices, use data for voter targeting, and receive data center training.
* Register Here: [https://secure.anedot.com/negop/virtual-bootcamp-3](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001L5SnTQVHUpPX7mLEx_McMAkZHJ4GX0QWs91Qd3chw9BAyIPJGuFDbtKyQ59zudX1AiNQ5AS8u4SZ6Rg86um-f0ZPEAWvzsww1W-GmZ7MNu00NdZUZHEYQYNKJ8O0tFt_EQ-hb0U6s2bMDrYHeVYZGm6mr0Je-yr950pAjVsR_6AmObh7TkgK3wfoYXJ0DtH8&c=5rE71rHX3DSKEJjx6tnDVHflID_LKAxSZdfrl2LPR_ILC9GYNg9sDg==&ch=z7jHy4y8gtzz3LB0BLijVuymloYvcrhrmELR7AMRRwgAYTPUtDz0KQ==)

November 13, 2025 at 6pm - Training 4: Political - The Ground Game and Putting it All Together
* Build grassroots infrastructure, execute voter contact strategies, manage mail campaigns, implement absentee voting, early voting, and Get Out The Vote (GOTV) efforts to mobilize voters, along with election integrity.
* Register Here:[ https://secure.anedot.com/negop/virtual-bootcamp-4](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001L5SnTQVHUpPX7mLEx_McMAkZHJ4GX0QWs91Qd3chw9BAyIPJGuFDbtKyQ59zudX13Tdr-x0niumnqvXKrhw98Fpd7-Dbq9M8aMEGugzvXTV1AZG1DghdWrs_UUCAGn9SMiYd9otnZMjIYzQDSgUW0K3JfjhlEPbep03g01GDjNNKlAnFhofMF-iVnQGPpQP0&c=5rE71rHX3DSKEJjx6tnDVHflID_LKAxSZdfrl2LPR_ILC9GYNg9sDg==&ch=z7jHy4y8gtzz3LB0BLijVuymloYvcrhrmELR7AMRRwgAYTPUtDz0KQ==)

If you want to win, skip the guesswork - this is where it starts. Training, tools, and the roadmap to victory.
If you have questions, please reach out to Joe Hagerty at [political@ne.gop](mailto:political@ne.gop)
Sincerely, Joe Hagerty Political Director Nebraska Republican Party
---
---
| | | | NEGOP | 1610 N St. | Lincoln, NE 68508 US
---

name: The Turning Point USA Family
subject: Charlie always believed this day would come
body:
[Mark as Spam](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?xul=6zs1j7t5dhe4avz97uxx7mw1o6ljglb6nw30o1eh8y9mj2a8mve8i1vlvthpa4ekjeaesmlmiuguiqfagre09jpdacmsqtbnsmrmearv4b97n4ugx7&__stmp=t3tbxq&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh) | [Unsubscribe](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?xul=6zs1j7t5dhe4avz97uxx7mw1o6ljglb6nw30o1eh8y9mj2a8mve8i1vlvthpa4ekjeaesmlmiuguiqfagre09jpdacmsqtbnsmrmearv4b97n4ugx7&__stmp=t3tbxq&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh)
---
Please read the message below from Turning Point USA.
* * *
| | | | [](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
Friend, Even now... it still doesn't feel real that we lost Charlie. His voice, his courage, his conviction — it shaped all of us. And his vision — **to build the most faithful and the most patriotic generation in American history** — is why Turning Point USA exists today. Friend, that's why we have an inspiring update for you... In just the first seven days since Charlie was taken from all of us, Turning Point USA has received more than 54,000 requests from students who want to start or join a chapter:
---
[](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
**God is so good.** ✝️🙏🕊️ To give you perspective, Friend... Coming into this school year, Turning Point USA had roughly 900+ college chapters and 1,200+ high school chapters. It took us years to build those chapters... over a decade in fact... _But now?_ These students who signed up? They weren't asked to... They weren't recruited... **They felt it was their time to stand up for this country and honor Charlie.** Because of the life he lived. The faith he had. The love he showed God, his family, and others. The courage he had to speak truth. And his vision of a better America for us all.
---
[](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
He always believed this day would come. He used to talk about it all the time — how the momentum would shift, how young people would rise up with courage, and how a Turning Point USA chapter would be in every college and high school in America. And now... _It's happening._ Faster than he could have imagined. Bigger than we thought possible. And more beautiful than we can even describe. **Again, Friend, God is so good.** Since we lost Charlie, we've also felt SO MUCH love... Prayers. Emails. Messages. Stories about how Charlie's voice changed lives... or your children's or grandchildren's lives. And so many supporters have asked, _"What can I do?"_ As you read this, our team is working around the clock to follow up with every chapter request. And we're going to need help equipping them with our activism kits. **Every new chapter receives these kits from Turning Point USA with everything they need to get started — including:** ✅ Leadership materials
✅ Pocket Constitutions
✅ Chapter training tools
✅ American flags & patriotic gear Friend, I'm sure you'd believe it when we say it... _this won't be a small task._ But we're going to do it. Whatever it takes. **_For Charlie._** _And here's how YOU can help with this..._ **Every $25 you give today helps stock another activism kit — ready to deploy into a new Turning Point USA student chapter...** How many of these activism kits can you help provide to the wave of new student chapters that are just getting started?
---
[ **1 KIT = $25**](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
[ **2 KITS = $50**](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
[ **10 KITS = $250**](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
[ **100 KITS = $2,500**](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
[ **1,000 KITS = $25,000**](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
[ **Sponsor Activism Kits**](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
Friend, if there was ever a more immediate way you could help make Charlie's vision a reality... **This is it****.**
---
[](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
Also... **Here's one more powerful thing you can do:** 📩 If you have a son, daughter, grandchild, niece, nephew — or any young person in your life — who wants to get involved... who wants to join or start a chapter... **Please send them this link:** **[TPUSAStudents.com/StartaChapter](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?xtl=71kqox8yyg1neegaz7m19r4hoakdlv1mqgj8853hzc06pf37zgjnufuk1nm1wpx9nb0bkucq6dmtrwnlkdw6ru2a6owel3l32kkhzdau3kkch0bghz0keis4jmoyhqj8nxs7qvmvpcnglbn2vcxh&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)** Friend, if you've already given... If you've already donated... If you've already been a voice of encouragement and strength in this painful week... If you've prayed, been praying, and continue to pray... **Thank you.** We can never say it enough. If you've read this far, we want you to know this... **You're the reason Charlie's mission and vision will grow for generations to come.** God is so good.
---
**For Charlie,** -The Turning Point USA Family **P.S.** Please know, Friend, if you can't afford to help right now, all we ask for are your prayers. Charlie would have wanted you to pray first — and if that's all you can give today, _we're grateful._ But if you're able to help... if you're overjoyed to hear that students are flocking to Charlie's message and mission... Your gift today helps sustain this wave of student inspiration and gets new student chapters off the ground floor... And ensures Charlie's vision is realized faster than we all thought possible. **Every $25 you give today helps us stock another activism kit ready to deploy to a new student chapter.**
---
[ **Sponsor an Activism Kit**
**Make Your Gift Here**](http://trk.americanmediawatchdog.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3tbxq&__onlt=h)
---
Thank you for your help and for your prayers, Friend. **God is so good.** 🙏
---
4940 East Beverly Road
Phoenix, Arizona 85044
---

clean_body: | Please read the message below from Turning Point USA. | | | | Friend, Even now... it still doesn't feel real that we lost Charlie. His voice, his courage, his conviction — it shaped all of us. And his vision — to build the most faithful and the most patriotic generation in American history — is why Turning Point USA exists today. Friend, that's why we have an inspiring update for you... In just the first seven days since Charlie was taken from all of us, Turning Point USA has received more than 54,000 requests from students who want to start or join a chapter: God is so good. ✝️🙏🕊️ To give you perspective, Friend... Coming into this school year, Turning Point USA had roughly 900+ college chapters and 1,200+ high school chapters. It took us years to build those chapters... over a decade in fact... But now? These students who signed up? They weren't asked to... They weren't recruited... They felt it was their time to stand up for this country and honor Charlie. Because of the life he lived. The faith he had. The love he showed God, his family, and others. The courage he had to speak truth. And his vision of a better America for us all. He always believed this day would come. He used to talk about it all the time — how the momentum would shift, how young people would rise up with courage, and how a Turning Point USA chapter would be in every college and high school in America. And now... It's happening. Faster than he could have imagined. Bigger than we thought possible. And more beautiful than we can even describe. Again, Friend, God is so good. Since we lost Charlie, we've also felt SO MUCH love... Prayers. Emails. Messages. Stories about how Charlie's voice changed lives... or your children's or grandchildren's lives. And so many supporters have asked, "What can I do?" As you read this, our team is working around the clock to follow up with every chapter request. And we're going to need help equipping them with our activism kits. Every new chapter receives these kits from Turning Point USA with everything they need to get started — including: ✅ Leadership materials ✅ Pocket Constitutions ✅ Chapter training tools ✅ American flags & patriotic gear Friend, I'm sure you'd believe it when we say it... this won't be a small task. But we're going to do it. Whatever it takes. For Charlie. And here's how YOU can help with this... Every $25 you give today helps stock another activism kit — ready to deploy into a new Turning Point USA student chapter... How many of these activism kits can you help provide to the wave of new student chapters that are just getting started? Friend, if there was ever a more immediate way you could help make Charlie's vision a reality... This is it**. Also... Here's one more powerful thing you can do: 📩 If you have a son, daughter, grandchild, niece, nephew — or any young person in your life — who wants to get involved... who wants to join or start a chapter... Please send them this link: Friend, if you've already given... If you've already donated... If you've already been a voice of encouragement and strength in this painful week... If you've prayed, been praying, and continue to pray... Thank you. We can never say it enough. If you've read this far, we want you to know this... You're the reason Charlie's mission and vision will grow for generations to come. God is so good. For Charlie, -The Turning Point USA Family P.S. Please know, Friend, if you can't afford to help right now, all we ask for are your prayers. Charlie would have wanted you to pray first — and if that's all you can give today, we're grateful. But if you're able to help... if you're overjoyed to hear that students are flocking to Charlie's message and mission... Your gift today helps sustain this wave of student inspiration and gets new student chapters off the ground floor... And ensures Charlie's vision is realized faster than we all thought possible. Every $25 you give today helps us stock another activism kit ready to deploy to a new student chapter. Thank you for your help and for your prayers, Friend. God is so good. 🙏 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044 / / MATERIAL TIE DISCLOSURE: You should presume the sender of this email has an affiliate or other financial relationship with the entities or persons noted in this message and may be compensated for purchases made on associated websites. subject: How Democrats can win (Part 2)
body:
Voters continue to trust Democrats on health care. But health care means a lot more than just insurance coverage.

| |
---|---|---

# [How Democrats can win (Part 2)](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=174951078&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc0OTUxMDc4LCJpYXQiOjE3NTk5MjgyMDQsImV4cCI6MTc2MjUyMDIwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TBbBoJYMxwoBHYYuvnAxt_Wfo2eLKLSlmufKrGBDL-4)
### Voters continue to trust Democrats on health care. But health care means a lot more than just insurance coverage.
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
| Oct 8
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=174951078&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc0OTUxMDc4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1OTkyODIwNCwiZXhwIjoxNzYyNTIwMjA0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjQ0MDM5OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.gpK5ruKRngaDZE4hRZu13xl9ORyhEYBj9g3Gv0HNbz8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=174951078&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc0OTUxMDc4LCJpYXQiOjE3NTk5MjgyMDQsImV4cCI6MTc2MjUyMDIwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TBbBoJYMxwoBHYYuvnAxt_Wfo2eLKLSlmufKrGBDL-4&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=174951078&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc0OTUxMDc4LCJpYXQiOjE3NTk5MjgyMDQsImV4cCI6MTc2MjUyMDIwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TBbBoJYMxwoBHYYuvnAxt_Wfo2eLKLSlmufKrGBDL-4)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvYmVuc2FtdWVscy9wL2hvdy1kZW1vY3JhdHMtY2FuLXdpbi1wYXJ0LTI_.RRZ1KebbBailfvHG5bwcIiMC_uqpMKudVy5AZMnBflY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bensamuels/p/how-democrats-can-win-part-2?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

One of the many weird things about American politics is that our [government periodically shuts down](https://substack.com/redirect/4dd80023-9b38-4039-a897-94495e3b001c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which isn't something that happens [anywhere else in the world](https://substack.com/redirect/961741ed-7875-4f3d-b6f2-6b851e70b58f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).1
But that's [happening right now](https://substack.com/redirect/c55bff21-ea85-4fa4-98bb-76a7d6f0df29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and the who's-to-blame messaging war between Democrats and Republicans is afoot. Republicans are [blaming Democrats for being obstructionist](https://substack.com/redirect/00377a0a-95af-49c0-bbea-f4fee75a4209?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and Democrats are blaming Republicans for [stripping health care from Americans](https://substack.com/redirect/65514601-e693-4631-a7cf-def32f50cdd6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
We're still more than a year away from the '26 midterm elections; I don't think the politics of this particular shutdown will be relevant by then,2 and I don't think my commentary on the shutdown itself is all that interesting.
What is notable: **health care is a winning issue for Democrats** , and that goes far beyond protecting Medicare and keeping insurance premiums low. That's where Democrats have an opportunity.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTc0OTUxMDc4LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzU5OTI4MjA0LCJleHAiOjIwNzU1MDQyMDQsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.tTjON4AMihYR618QCMH08Zh8e3od5QGuDw1s4OcB2Pw?)
> _**Article summary:**_
> * _Health care is an issue where voters are already inclined to trust Democrats --and there aren't all that many issues where that's the case._
> * _Republicans are doing more than just slashing health insurance. Trump 's policy changes to everything from research grants to managing pregnancy are harmful._
> * _Democrats should expand the debate around health care: make it an issue about fighting pediatric cancers and finding cures for dementia. Those are winning issues!_
>

### Voters actually trust Democrats on health care
Trump [is not especially popular](https://substack.com/redirect/5344c1f6-3ccf-4e40-8969-2445f9ea3090?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), as [I noted two weeks ago](https://substack.com/redirect/d901a799-829c-4824-9924-b6b4d3e136db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The problem is that Democrats are also unpopular, [more so than they've been in decades](https://substack.com/redirect/ecc67113-d01d-4320-9e23-603f055643dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
But when it comes to health care, it's a different story. Among the issues most important to voters (on the right half of the chart below), _[every single issue](https://substack.com/redirect/502fb86e-4e4b-4dde-99ee-72880c2d8c77?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ where voters trust Democrats more than Republicans](https://substack.com/redirect/502fb86e-4e4b-4dde-99ee-72880c2d8c77?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) relates to health care and public health.3
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/28c21d3e-c98c-4310-a7e1-82faa7db1c67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: [Blue Rose Research tracking surveys as of February 2025](https://substack.com/redirect/502fb86e-4e4b-4dde-99ee-72880c2d8c77?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Across the board, Democrats have a lot of work to do.4 But it's nice when you already come to an issue with an advantage, and that's clearly the case on health care.
### "Health care" should go beyond just insurance premiums and coverage
When we discuss health care in this country, it usually involves things like Medicaid coverage, health insurance premiums, medical debt, etc.
I'll say two things on that. First, because of the recent [One Big Beautiful Bill Act](https://substack.com/redirect/6f7575c9-1e9a-48c1-9f56-5009e328cebb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), these are real issues:
* Over the next decade, the number of uninsured Americans will increase by [7.8 million](https://substack.com/redirect/1034ac3b-ed5b-4215-836d-382d511d3c70?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to [10 million people](https://substack.com/redirect/6892528c-bec3-4003-aee5-e838528c205a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), including [160,000 more uninsured people](https://substack.com/redirect/b244099b-8df8-4ea1-b0a9-b4ff6b5c2202?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Missouri.
* Out-of-pocket5 [health insurance premiums will double](https://substack.com/redirect/dec5b393-5949-49e2-a9e3-d9fa9b3f61b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for many Americans who get their coverage through the [ACA exchange](https://substack.com/redirect/2c31cad8-029c-4cae-9f70-26d4ff89d11c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). That will [hit Republican districts harder](https://substack.com/redirect/e2854972-67a6-4030-84cf-5cec7a48a433?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than Democratic ones.
* "[Uncompensated care](https://substack.com/redirect/e6c26069-efe4-4c98-99dc-2c7130509da8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)," defined as services that don't get reimbursed, will increase by about [$200 billion over the next decade](https://substack.com/redirect/a7655240-7e4a-444b-b6b6-6e52ae3febae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Medical practices and hospital systems will have to cover more of those expenses, which in practice leads to worse patient care and reduced access to medical treatments.

To state the obvious, all of this is bad.
My second point: it's already the marquee issue of this latest government shutdown. _Lots_ of people are already talking about this, and I don't have much to add.
But what the Trump administration is saying and doing on the topic of health care goes way beyond just insurance coverage.
They're making Americans less healthy, less safe, and less comfortable. All of this should be called out aggressively; Americans are against what Trump is doing here.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=174951078&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc0OTUxMDc4LCJpYXQiOjE3NTk5MjgyMDQsImV4cCI6MTc2MjUyMDIwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TBbBoJYMxwoBHYYuvnAxt_Wfo2eLKLSlmufKrGBDL-4)
### We're cutting research for cancer and dementia
Not long after the election last year, I wrote about one of the greatest public health accomplishments in the last quarter century: [the reduction in deaths from cancer](https://substack.com/redirect/5431e6a9-6ebc-435a-b739-da6e8fc49512?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[](https://substack.com/redirect/b8809b74-6351-4504-b26f-a89b44123416?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |
#### [What to feel good about right now](https://substack.com/redirect/b8809b74-6351-4504-b26f-a89b44123416?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/profile/12425779-ben-samuels) and [Julie Roland](https://substack.com/profile/91253051-julie-roland)| | *
---|---|---
| November 18, 2024
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/b8809b74-6351-4504-b26f-a89b44123416?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
There are a lot of reasons why the death rate from cancer is declining, but one of them: the U.S. federal government has collaborated closely with universities for decades.
People speak abstractly about how universities have partnered with the federal government, so I think it's important to call out specifically the sorts of discoveries that have saved and improved lives in the U.S. (and around the world).6
* The [Human Genome Project](https://substack.com/redirect/fbeb25b9-d9e7-4427-994f-dda9af9af679?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which involved [research at universities across the U.S.](https://substack.com/redirect/aac4f3a1-2046-4624-97b4-07d378b8ad46?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), unlocked an entirely [new field of medicine called genomics](https://substack.com/redirect/fd0da223-06bd-4ae8-805c-4cd9f1b28608?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that's used for all sorts of novel, highly effective medical treatments.
* Immunotherapy, now common in treating lots of cancers, came out of [research that began at universities](https://substack.com/redirect/143d184e-44db-4342-b704-7ba6a1c64a8a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). [mRNA vaccines](https://substack.com/redirect/2ddae569-a1c4-43bb-b9ef-32fa57d9d8e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), including the COVID vaccine that saved millions of lives, are possible because of university-led research too.
* All sorts of different medications and treatments--everything from [Keytruda](https://substack.com/redirect/8fad48c3-cb30-4f67-9b82-d0256b8f694a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (a cancer treatment) to Ozempic to Viagra7--[came out of the research partnership](https://substack.com/redirect/139de97a-4bc5-44db-8864-7f05345fad86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) between the federal government and universities.8

And all of this is--or was, anyway--[ongoing](https://substack.com/redirect/620ebbc1-559a-4870-9869-5ee4b8d2b4b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): research on everything from cancer to autoimmune diseases to neurodegenerative diseases.
But a lot of this funding is now being threatened, partially because of DOGE and partially because of the Trump administration's war against American universities.
I wouldn't suggest that Democrats pick the fight to stand up for universities, per se: the approval rating for [higher education is pretty low](https://substack.com/redirect/7f97d357-c506-4a8b-a9aa-beb74eab48be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [especially among Republicans](https://substack.com/redirect/19434a0f-63ac-4811-9ba3-e42051e275e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
But you know what Americans _don 't_ want? A federal government that cuts research grants used to find treatments for [pediatric brain tumors](https://substack.com/redirect/201c2900-46a8-4bdd-be54-73788230b1cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [diabetes](https://substack.com/redirect/88654a2c-7cb7-4515-8f92-f3c2249032ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [infectious diseases](https://substack.com/redirect/f102043c-3b4e-468a-b202-71a418e16d18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), or that find ways to support [people with dementia](https://substack.com/redirect/0b7102ea-b1b4-48fc-b202-d38e1e3d5aba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or [children with autism](https://substack.com/redirect/88654a2c-7cb7-4515-8f92-f3c2249032ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Unfortunately, all of that is happening.
Democrats should go after Republicans on this issue, especially when there's still so much--like [why cancers for people under 50 are fast increasing](https://substack.com/redirect/8c23e3a8-5035-4d79-8c86-953f1c11e0b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--that's alarming and remains unanswered.
### "Tough it out" and the diminishment of pain and discomfort
I know, nothing controversial that Trump says ever seems to stick. But I really thought [this one](https://substack.com/redirect/f8b13253-fe19-43ac-bff6-7391f2fd06db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) might:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/f8b13253-fe19-43ac-bff6-7391f2fd06db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Here's the transcript of what he said (emphasis added):
> Tylenol during pregnancy can be associated with a very increased risk of autism. So taking Tylenol is, uh, not good. Alright, I'll say it: it's not good. For this reason, they are strongly recommending that women limit Tylenol use during pregnancy unless medically necessary. That's, for instance, in cases of extremely high fever that **you feel you can 't tough it out, you can't do it. I guess there's that. It's a small number of cases, I think. But if you can't tough it out, if you can't do it**, that's what you're going to have to do. You'll take a Tylenol, but it'll be very sparingly.
I'm not going to weigh in on the (blatantly wrong) scientific assertions here. I'll leave it to the [American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists](https://substack.com/redirect/e8b308f5-937c-4a82-a49f-0e5b9c4676a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or [Cleveland Clinic](https://substack.com/redirect/64b016f6-353b-48d2-adaf-dbd87a02f0b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or the [Harvard Medical School](https://substack.com/redirect/2d147b7d-ac90-49cc-aa1b-cb982b1ed23d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to tell you that it's safe.9
What I'll focus on instead: pregnancy is hard. [70%-80% of women](https://substack.com/redirect/072f7501-5e74-43e5-8aa3-dd660309822d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) experience nausea during pregnancy and [70% experience back pain](https://substack.com/redirect/ae34e19c-2170-40c9-a3a4-35a4ec621121?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). [Heartburn](https://substack.com/redirect/a56da45a-1348-49ce-a527-dd6a4d1d75ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [insomnia](https://substack.com/redirect/51be82e0-54a6-481d-93a0-090676a212c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [anxiety](https://substack.com/redirect/65e921b8-31fe-4568-991b-2641d2310ea8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are also extremely common.10
So to be dismissive of pain, or to say that pregnancy just requires "toughening it out," is insane--and a political opportunity.
I've written lots about how [Democrats can win back men](https://substack.com/redirect/0003f38e-3cd5-4331-b282-0f9fe1735ae5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but young women are an issue for Democrats too. They [moved to the right in '24](https://substack.com/redirect/82ac6564-d377-41ee-a2e0-ce549b4a7bb5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), albeit not by nearly as much as men did.
[Abortion is generally a good issue for Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/ccae5147-60c5-4594-9644-79883d6b422d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but it's also fraught, charged, and intensely political. Here's a chance to talk to women of all stripes--pro- and anti-abortion--about being supportive of parents, parenthood, and pregnancy.
Using the miracle of modern science to make pregnancy more comfortable and safe should be a nonpartisan issue. Federal policies are heading the other way: they're conspiratorial, they're wrong, and they'll lead to needless pain and suffering.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=174951078&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc0OTUxMDc4LCJpYXQiOjE3NTk5MjgyMDQsImV4cCI6MTc2MjUyMDIwNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TBbBoJYMxwoBHYYuvnAxt_Wfo2eLKLSlmufKrGBDL-4)
### Vaccines
And speaking of absolute quackery coming out of the federal administration, let's touch on vaccine policy. I've written about this a bunch already:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/75627cbb-3f8e-480e-a520-d91ea185abff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |
#### [Vaccines on offense](https://substack.com/redirect/75627cbb-3f8e-480e-a520-d91ea185abff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/profile/12425779-ben-samuels)| | *
---|---|---
| Jun 18
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/75627cbb-3f8e-480e-a520-d91ea185abff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
I won't rehash all of this, but here's what I will say: even as support for vaccines [moves the wrong way](https://substack.com/redirect/98be5cf6-698a-4707-b902-3d2481274010?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), lots of Americans remain undecided and persuadable:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/98be5cf6-698a-4707-b902-3d2481274010?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: [Gallup](https://substack.com/redirect/98be5cf6-698a-4707-b902-3d2481274010?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), from July 2024
It's extremely unsettling how many people are undecided on this issue, but it's why we have to go on offense here--not only because it's the right thing to do, but because there's political opportunity too.11
Measles outbreaks [like the one in West Texas](https://substack.com/redirect/5baa23eb-0404-407b-ab0b-7261f43573f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are, after all, totally preventable. But the Trump administration doesn't care to take the basic steps that we know work.
### Broadening a winning issue
I do think calling out Republican policies that increase insurance costs and reduce coverage is the right leading message for Democrats--it's very easy to point the finger at Republicans on those issues.
But the opportunity doesn't end there. **What the Trump administration is doing is unempathetic, demonstrably bad for public health, and out of touch with what Americans want**. It's time to be more forceful in calling all of that out--and making Republican candidates around the country take stances on these issues too.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTc0OTUxMDc4LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzU5OTI4MjA0LCJleHAiOjIwNzU1MDQyMDQsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.tTjON4AMihYR618QCMH08Zh8e3od5QGuDw1s4OcB2Pw?)
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/7fa41e80-8973-4b20-a299-5ad4ae04c9f8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)._
1
That's why, amidst [this latest shutdown](https://substack.com/redirect/c55bff21-ea85-4fa4-98bb-76a7d6f0df29?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), 1.5 million government employees--everyone from [customer support for Social Security](https://substack.com/redirect/95e4f9d6-a645-4fbe-bd16-6b69b1ab7a6e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to [National Park rangers](https://substack.com/redirect/63d875d1-e6e3-49c7-8cbc-56f31b869115?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--aren't getting paid.
2
If it's still relevant in a year, then we better brace ourselves for a _very_ long shutdown.
3
Health care is the [third-strongest issue for Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/ccae5147-60c5-4594-9644-79883d6b422d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), behind only LGBTQ issues and the environment. There's a bit of nuance in how that's defined, so let's dig into exactly what that means for a moment.
Here's how [that poll](https://substack.com/redirect/ccae5147-60c5-4594-9644-79883d6b422d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is worded exactly: "Which party do you think does a better job handling" and then lists a bunch of issues. So when I say "strongest," I don't mean that in the sense that it necessarily has the power to persuade people, but rather that when pointedly asked about these policies, voters are more inclined to trust Democrats than Republicans.
If you're curious, the three worst issues (i.e., where voters trust Republicans most vs. Democrats) are 1) crime, 2) the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, and 3) immigration.
4
On the three issues voters care about most--"cost of living," "the economy," and "inflation"--they trust Republicans much more than Democrats.
Note that this data is about eight months old now, so I would imagine there have been some shifts here--especially on inflation, where I think Trump will start to get hit harder.
5
Fun fact: "out-of-pocket" [means something totally different to Gen Z](https://substack.com/redirect/fe666729-8d2e-49a0-bf80-b8b4c335964d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than it does to the rest of us.
6
If you go back even further in time, the list of achievements is incredible. Until the late '40s, we didn't really understand that high blood pressure and high cholesterol led to cardiovascular disease and premature death. [University research changed that](https://substack.com/redirect/0d67bc50-c377-4f0f-802e-8fe7f0f7c6ad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
7
Not ascertainable data: how many Congressmen (and I'm using "men" correctly in this instance) support slashing research funding but are themselves beneficiaries of the research that led to Viagra. I suspect it's not zero!
8
The lives improved and saved by this funding are immeasurable. If you need any external validation: 174 people whose research has been supported by the National Institutes of Health [have won Nobel Prizes](https://substack.com/redirect/4c12a590-c638-49f1-8241-8eaa9b6eb06f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
It's not the main thrust of the argument, but it's an important one: these research dollars have historically brought some of the best and brightest from around the world to research and teach at our universities. A lot of those people will likely end up moving elsewhere in the world now.
9
Tylenol isn't just safe: it can be critical, in fact, [for pregnant women with fevers](https://substack.com/redirect/bb57566b-761b-4b73-b524-1a80a61a751c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
10
I suspect "pregnancy is hard" is not something that needs citing for women who've actually been pregnant. But I want to put the data out there anyway, so the ignorant out there (for instance, me) can understand how hard it is.
11
Lots of people who elected Trump to be tough on immigration or fight inflation [aren't enthused by what Bobby Kennedy, Jr. is up to](https://substack.com/redirect/d29e3858-5ec0-4e0e-9450-8b786d77e516?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
If I had to guess--and it is just that--his approval rating will continue to decline as Americans get to know him better. The more he's in the public eye, the easier it is to see that he's nuts.

But health care means a lot more than just insurance coverage. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Voters continue to trust Democrats on health care. But health care means a lot more than just insurance coverage. | | | Oct 8 | | | | | | | | | | | | One of the many weird things about American politics is that our , which isn't something that happens But that's , and the who's-to-blame messaging war between Democrats and Republicans is afoot. Republicans are , and Democrats are blaming Republicans for . We're still more than a year away from the '26 midterm elections; I don't think the politics of this particular shutdown will be relevant by then,2 and I don't think my commentary on the shutdown itself is all that interesting. What is notable: health care is a winning issue for Democrats , and that goes far beyond protecting Medicare and keeping insurance premiums low. That's where Democrats have an opportunity. ? Article summary: Health care is an issue where voters are already inclined to trust Democrats --and there aren't all that many issues where that's the case. Republicans are doing more than just slashing health insurance. Trump 's policy changes to everything from research grants to managing pregnancy are harmful. Democrats should expand the debate around health care: make it an issue about fighting pediatric cancers and finding cures for dementia. Those are winning issues! Voters actually trust Democrats on health care Trump , as . The problem is that Democrats are also unpopular, . But when it comes to health care, it's a different story. Among the issues most important to voters (on the right half of the chart below), relates to health care and public health.3 | | ---|---|--- Source: Across the board, Democrats have a lot of work to do.4 But it's nice when you already come to an issue with an advantage, and that's clearly the case on health care. "Health care" should go beyond just insurance premiums and coverage When we discuss health care in this country, it usually involves things like Medicaid coverage, health insurance premiums, medical debt, etc. I'll say two things on that. First, because of the recent , these are real issues: Over the next decade, the number of uninsured Americans will increase by to , including in Missouri. Out-of-pocket5 for many Americans who get their coverage through the . That will than Democratic ones. "," defined as services that don't get reimbursed, will increase by about . Medical practices and hospital systems will have to cover more of those expenses, which in practice leads to worse patient care and reduced access to medical treatments. To state the obvious, all of this is bad. My second point: it's already the marquee issue of this latest government shutdown. Lots of people are already talking about this, and I don't have much to add. But what the Trump administration is saying and doing on the topic of health care goes way beyond just insurance coverage. They're making Americans less healthy, less safe, and less comfortable. All of this should be called out aggressively; Americans are against what Trump is doing here. We're cutting research for cancer and dementia Not long after the election last year, I wrote about one of the greatest public health accomplishments in the last quarter century: . | | | | | and | | ---|---|--- | November 18, 2024 | There are a lot of reasons why the death rate from cancer is declining, but one of them: the U.S. federal government has collaborated closely with universities for decades. People speak abstractly about how universities have partnered with the federal government, so I think it's important to call out specifically the sorts of discoveries that have saved and improved lives in the U.S. (and around the world).6 The , which involved , unlocked an entirely that's used for all sorts of novel, highly effective medical treatments. Immunotherapy, now common in treating lots of cancers, came out of . , including the COVID vaccine that saved millions of lives, are possible because of university-led research too. All sorts of different medications and treatments--everything from (a cancer treatment) to Ozempic to Viagra7-- between the federal government and universities.8 And all of this is--or was, anyway--: research on everything from cancer to autoimmune diseases to neurodegenerative diseases. But a lot of this funding is now being threatened, partially because of DOGE and partially because of the Trump administration's war against American universities. I wouldn't suggest that Democrats pick the fight to stand up for universities, per se: the approval rating for , . But you know what Americans don 't want? A federal government that cuts research grants used to find treatments for , , and , or that find ways to support or . Unfortunately, all of that is happening. Democrats should go after Republicans on this issue, especially when there's still so much--like alarming and remains unanswered. "Tough it out" and the diminishment of pain and discomfort I know, nothing controversial that Trump says ever seems to stick. But I really thought might: Here's the transcript of what he said (emphasis added): Tylenol during pregnancy can be associated with a very increased risk of autism. So taking Tylenol is, uh, not good. Alright, I'll say it: it's not good. For this reason, they are strongly recommending that women limit Tylenol use during pregnancy unless medically necessary. That's, for instance, in cases of extremely high fever that you feel you can 't tough it out, you can't do it. I guess there's that. It's a small number of cases, I think. But if you can't tough it out, if you can't do it, that's what you're going to have to do. You'll take a Tylenol, but it'll be very sparingly. I'm not going to weigh in on the (blatantly wrong) scientific assertions here. I'll leave it to the or or the to tell you that it's safe.9 What I'll focus on instead: pregnancy is hard. experience nausea during pregnancy and . , , and are also extremely common.10 So to be dismissive of pain, or to say that pregnancy just requires "toughening it out," is insane--and a political opportunity. I've written lots about how , but young women are an issue for Democrats too. They , albeit not by nearly as much as men did. , but it's also fraught, charged, and intensely political. Here's a chance to talk to women of all stripes--pro- and anti-abortion--about being supportive of parents, parenthood, and pregnancy. Using the miracle of modern science to make pregnancy more comfortable and safe should be a nonpartisan issue. Federal policies are heading the other way: they're conspiratorial, they're wrong, and they'll lead to needless pain and suffering. Vaccines And speaking of absolute quackery coming out of the federal administration, let's touch on vaccine policy. I've written about this a bunch already: | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | Jun 18 | I won't rehash all of this, but here's what I will say: even as support for vaccines , lots of Americans remain undecided and persuadable: | | ---|---|--- Source: , from July 2024 It's extremely unsettling how many people are undecided on this issue, but it's why we have to go on offense here--not only because it's the right thing to do, but because there's political opportunity too.11 Measles outbreaks are, after all, totally preventable. But the Trump administration doesn't care to take the basic steps that we know work. Broadening a winning issue I do think calling out Republican policies that increase insurance costs and reduce coverage is the right leading message for Democrats--it's very easy to point the finger at Republicans on those issues. But the opportunity doesn't end there. What the Trump administration is doing is unempathetic, demonstrably bad for public health, and out of touch with what Americans want. It's time to be more forceful in calling all of that out--and making Republican candidates around the country take stances on these issues too. ? Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, . 1 That's why, amidst , 1.5 million government employees--everyone from to getting paid. 2 If it's still relevant in a year, then we better brace ourselves for a very long shutdown. 3 Health care is the , behind only LGBTQ issues and the environment. There's a bit of nuance in how that's defined, so let's dig into exactly what that means for a moment. Here's how is worded exactly: "Which party do you think does a better job handling" and then lists a bunch of issues. So when I say "strongest," I don't mean that in the sense that it necessarily has the power to persuade people, but rather that when pointedly asked about these policies, voters are more inclined to trust Democrats than Republicans. If you're curious, the three worst issues (i.e., where voters trust Republicans most vs. Democrats) are 1) crime, 2) the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, and 3) immigration. 4 On the three issues voters care about most--"cost of living," "the economy," and "inflation"--they trust Republicans much more than Democrats. Note that this data is about eight months old now, so I would imagine there have been some shifts here--especially on inflation, where I think Trump will start to get hit harder. 5 Fun fact: "out-of-pocket" than it does to the rest of us. 6 If you go back even further in time, the list of achievements is incredible. Until the late '40s, we didn't really understand that high blood pressure and high cholesterol led to cardiovascular disease and premature death. . 7 Not ascertainable data: how many Congressmen (and I'm using "men" correctly in this instance) support slashing research funding but are themselves beneficiaries of the research that led to Viagra. I suspect it's not zero! 8 The lives improved and saved by this funding are immeasurable. If you need any external validation: 174 people whose research has been supported by the National Institutes of Health . It's not the main thrust of the argument, but it's an important one: these research dollars have historically brought some of the best and brightest from around the world to research and teach at our universities. A lot of those people will likely end up moving elsewhere in the world now. 9 Tylenol isn't just safe: it can be critical, in fact, . 10 I suspect "pregnancy is hard" is not something that needs citing for women who've actually been pregnant. But I want to put the data out there anyway, so the ignorant out there (for instance, me) can understand how hard it is. 11 Lots of people who elected Trump to be tough on immigration or fight inflation . If I had to guess--and it is just that--his approval rating will continue to decline as Americans get to know him better. The more he's in the public eye, the easier it is to see that he's nuts. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Ben Samuels 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

name: Lily Tang Williams
subject: LILY TANG WILLIAMS URGING CFIUS AND NEW HAMPSHIRE LEGISLATURE TO TAKE ACTION
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/lilytangwilliams/lily-tang-williams-urging-cfius-and-new-hampshire-legislature-to-take-action?e=c338950e03)
---
| |
---
| |
#
**FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE**
Weare, New Hampshire
October 8, 2025
Contact: lily4congress@lilytangwilliams.com

**LILY TANG WILLIAMS URGING CFIUS AND NEW HAMPSHIRE LEGISLATURE TO TAKE ACTION**

Federal agents from Homeland Security disrupted a potentially catastrophic cyber attack in New York and New Jersey. Military-grade hardware was discovered hidden in multiple clandestine locations consisting of hundreds of thousands of SIM cards and hardware. This was potentially another Chinese Communist Party asymmetric warfare plot - one that could have crippled NYC, jammed communications and 911 systems, and enabled secure communications among hostile agents already here.

Lily Tang Williams, Republican candidate for New Hampshire’s Second Congressional District who was born and grew up in China, said: "This is potentially very dangerous because this system is constructed in such a way that it could easily paralyze civilian infrastructure. The CCP is our largest adversary but we have allowed their infiltration into almost every aspect of our country."

Lily Tang Williams has been sounding alarm since early May on NongFu Spring quiet purchase of 80 Northwest Blvd, Nashua NH, an industrial property on 23 acres. She also warned Daniel Webster College next to Nashua Airport is owned by Chinese with a Drone company renting two buildings.

"Both of these deals must be reviewed by the Committee on Foreign Investment in the US (CFIUS) because they are within 100 miles of radius of Joint Base Cape Cod, one of the critical military installations listed on their website that requires investigation and approval of Federal Government. They are also near FAA traffic control center for the entire New England States, New Boston Space Force Station, several defense contractors and Pennichuck water pond that supplies water to the residents of southern NH."

Lily discovered that NongFu Spring, the largest beverage company in China, has formed partnership with a CCP propaganda group to promote the party narratives with dual logos on their water bottles sold in China, highlighting their relationship. Also the Chinese owner of Chester College of New England in New Hampshire is deeply connected with the CCP top leaders and Chinese military. "We have a new state law banning China and other adversary countries buying land here, but we must make this law retroactive to force these companies to divest these properties. I urge our state legislature to take action to investigate all of these land deals, and pass legislation to get the CCP out of our state. I believe most Granite Staters would concur."
---
